notes:
**New**

- **Move a line up or down with ⌘⇧↑ / ⌘⇧↓.** Reorders a bullet (or any indented
  line) among its siblings, carrying its sub-items with it — the outliner move
  you expect from Logseq/Roam. The cursor rides along. At the top/bottom of a
  group it pops the line out to its parent's level (up → above the parent, down →
  below it), so you can walk a line all the way out. (`BULLETS.md` §D1/§D2.)

**Fixes & polish**

- **Dark mode: inline `code` is readable again.** Inline code kept a light
  background in dark mode, leaving the text nearly invisible; it now follows the
  theme. No change in light mode.
- **Enter on a bullet that has sub-items no longer drags them into the new
  bullet.** Pressing Enter in a bullet with children now adds a fresh sub-item at
  the top of its list (carrying any text after the cursor into it) — matching the
  outliner behavior in Logseq — instead of moving all the existing sub-items under
  the new bullet. If the bullet is **collapsed**, the new bullet is added as a
  sibling after it (so it isn't hidden). (Part of an ongoing bullet-experience
  pass; see `BULLETS.md`.)
- **Backspace at the start of a bullet now merges it into the bullet above** (as
  in Logseq), instead of un-indenting it. A first sub-item with nothing above it
  still un-indents. Outdenting moves to Shift-Tab.
- **Shift-Tab on a bullet now pulls its lower siblings in as sub-items** (Logseq
  outliner behavior): un-indenting a bullet that has bullets below it makes those
  become its children, instead of leaving them behind.
- **These outliner behaviors now also apply to indented *paragraphs*, not just
  bullets** — Infiniti's "any block can be a child" structure (Tab to indent any
  line) now matches: Enter on an indented parent makes a proper first child
  (rather than an odd un-indented line between the parent and its children), and
  Shift-Tab pulls in lower siblings the same way.
- **Selecting up to the very top of a note now works.** Pressing the Up arrow
  (or Shift-Up to extend a selection) from an indented line could leave the
  cursor stranded at the end of the first line — so the first line's own text
  could never be reached or selected. Up / Shift-Up now land at the very start of
  the note. (`BULLETS.md` §S1.)
- **Merging bullets keeps everything on one line and never strands sub-items.**
  Backspacing at the start of a bullet that has sub-items used to drop its text
  onto a second line inside the bullet above (and, for indented paragraphs, could
  detach the sub-items); it now merges cleanly into one line and the sub-items
  move up as children of that bullet. (`BULLETS.md` §C5.)
- **Forward-Delete at the end of a bullet now pulls the next line up** (like
  Logseq): it merges in the first sub-item if the bullet has any (its own
  sub-items rise a level), otherwise the next bullet below — carrying that
  bullet's sub-items along. Works for indented paragraphs too. (`BULLETS.md` §C4.)
- **Collapsed bullets stay intact when editing around them.** Forward-Delete at
  the end of a collapsed bullet now merges the next visible line instead of
  yanking a hidden sub-item into view, and moving a collapsed bullet keeps it
  collapsed with its sub-items along for the ride. (`BULLETS.md` §E1.)
